Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants me to analyze an event log of an Insurance Claims Processing process My specific tasks are Identify cases with performance issues longer lead times Analyze attributes Resource Region Complexity to find root causes Propose explanations and mitigation strategies

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Analyze the Data Event Log I need to calculate the duration for each case to identify the slow ones

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2001 Start 2024 04 01 09 00 Submit Claim End 2024 04 01 10 30 Close Claim Duration 1 hour 30 minutes Attributes Region A Low Complexity Path Submit Evaluate Approve Pay Close Happy path no rework loops

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2002 Start 2024 04 01 09 05 Submit Claim End 2024 04 02 11 00 Close Claim Duration 26 hours Attributes Region B Medium Complexity Path Submit Evaluate Request Additional Documents Approve Pay Close Note One Request Additional Documents event

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2003 Start 2024 04 01 09 10 Submit Claim End 2024 04 03 09 30 Close Claim Duration 48 hours Attributes Region A High Complexity Path Submit Evaluate Request Additional Documents Request Additional Documents Approve Pay Close Note Multiple Request Additional Documents events 2 times

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2004 Start 2024 04 01 09 20 Submit Claim End 2024 04 01 10 45 Close Claim Duration 1 hour 25 minutes Attributes Region B Low Complexity Path Submit Evaluate Approve Pay Close Happy path

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2005 Start 2024 04 01 09 25 Submit Claim End 2024 04 04 14 30 Close Claim Duration 77 hours Attributes Region B High Complexity Path Submit Evaluate Request Additional Documents Request Additional Documents Request Additional Documents Approve Pay Close Note Multiple Request Additional Documents events 3 times This is the longest case

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Performance Duration Ranking Case 2004 1 4 hours Fastest Case 2001 1 5 hours Case 2002 26 hours Case 2003 48 hours Case 2005 77 hours Slowest

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Correlation with Complexity Low Complexity 2001 2004 1 5 hours average No additional documents requested Medium Complexity 2002 26 hours 1 request for documents High Complexity 2003 2005 48 to 77 hours Multiple requests for documents 2 to 3 times Insight There is a strong positive correlation between Complexity and Duration Higher complexity leads to Request Additional Documents loops significantly extending the process

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Correlation with Region Region A Cases 2001 Low 1 5h 2003 High 48h Region B Cases 2002 Medium 26h 2004 Low 1 4h 2005 High 77h Insight Region itself does not seem to be the primary driver of slowness independent of complexity

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: However looking at the slowest High Complexity case Case 2005 Region B 77h vs Case 2003 Region A 48h Region B seems to struggle more with High Complexity claims than Region A Why Resource analysis might help here

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Correlation with Resource Adjusters Adjuster Mike Region A handled 2001 Low and 2003 High 2003 took 48 hours had 2 document requests Adjuster Lisa Region B handled 2002 Medium 2004 Low and 2005 High 2005 took 77 hours had 3 document requests

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Managers Manager Ann handled 2001 2002 2004 All relatively fast approvals Manager Bill handled 2003 High and 2005 High Both high complexity cases went to Manager Bill

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The bottleneck seems to be before the manager during the document collection phase by the adjusters but Manager Bill only gets the complex slow cases

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The Request Additional Documents Loop This is the main driver of delay

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 2005 Evaluate 04 01 09 50 Request 1 04 01 11 30 Request 2 04 02 17 00 Request 3 04 03 15 00 This suggests an iterative struggle to get documents

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypothesis Adjuster Lisa might be struggling with High Complexity claims compared to Adjuster Mike 77h vs 48h for similar complexity or Region B process for gathering documents is less efficient

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Refinement of Data Analysis Let us double check the math

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Comparison Mike A gathered what he needed in 1 day 2 requests on same day Lisa B dragged it out over 3 days requests spread out This suggests Lisa Region B has a performance issue specifically with handling the documentation gathering for high complexity claims The gaps between requests are huge

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Approval Bottleneck check Manager Bill Last doc req to Approval gap is 19 to 23 hours Overnight wait Manager Ann Evaluation to Approval gap Low complexity cases Case 2001 Eval 09 30 to Appr 10 00 30 mins Case 2004 Eval 09 35 to Appr 10 00 25 mins Case 2002 Req Docs 14 00 to Approve 10 00 next day Gap 20 hours

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This implies the approval step has a systematic delay for cases requiring documents or Manager Ann processed it next morning

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Is Bill the bottleneck Or is it just the nature of approving complex claims The gap for Bill 19 to 23h is similar to Anns gap for the Medium case 20h Conclusion The approval stage introduces a next day delay for any case that is not instant but the major delay in Case 2005 is the document gathering phase Adjuster Lisa

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Part 1 Performance Identification Calculate throughput times Rank 2005 Worst 2003 2002 2001 2004

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Part 2 Root Cause Analysis Complexity is the main driver Low 1 5h High 48h to 77h Activity Request Additional Documents is the delay culprit It implies rework or waiting for external parties

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Adjuster Lisa Region B is associated with the worst performance Case 2005 Her document requests were spread over 3 days Adjuster Mike Region A handled high complexity faster Case 2003 finishing document needs in 1 day Manager Bill processes complex claims the approval step adds about 20 hours likely due to batching or workload whereas Manager Ann handles simpler claims quickly

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Recommendations Adjuster training Lisa needs support Pre emptive document lists for High complexity Manager availability Batching review
